chapter_title: Gen. Provisions Relating to Vehicle Size & Weight
text:
(a) The operator or owner of a vehicle is not required to unload any part of the vehicle’s load under Section 621.403 (Unloading Vehicle if Gross Weight Exceeded) or 621.404 (Unloading Vehicle if Axle Weight Exceeded) if the vehicle is:

(1) a motor vehicle loaded with timber, pulp wood, or agricultural products in their natural state being transported from the place of production to the place of marketing or first processing; or

(2) a vehicle crossing a highway as provided by Subchapter C (Contract Allowing Oversize or Overweight Vehicle to Cross Road; Surety Bond) , Chapter 623 (Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles) .

(b) The operator of a motor vehicle may proceed to the vehicle’s destination without unloading the vehicle as required by Section 621.403 (Unloading Vehicle if Gross Weight Exceeded) or 621.404 (Unloading Vehicle if Axle Weight Exceeded) if the vehicle is loaded with livestock.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 621. Gen. Provisions Relating to Vehicle Size & Weight
##### § 621.405 — Unloading Exceptions

(a) The operator or owner of a vehicle is not required to unload any part of the vehicle’s load under Section 621.403 (Unloading Vehicle if Gross Weight Exceeded) or 621.404 (Unloading Vehicle if Axle Weight Exceeded) if the vehicle is:

    (1) a motor vehicle loaded with timber, pulp wood, or agricultural products in their natural state being transported from the place of production to the place of marketing or first processing; or

    (2) a vehicle crossing a highway as provided by Subchapter C (Contract Allowing Oversize or Overweight Vehicle to Cross Road; Surety Bond) , Chapter 623 (Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles) .

(b) The operator of a motor vehicle may proceed to the vehicle’s destination without unloading the vehicle as required by Section 621.403 (Unloading Vehicle if Gross Weight Exceeded) or 621.404 (Unloading Vehicle if Axle Weight Exceeded) if the vehicle is loaded with livestock.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 2001, 77th Leg., ch. 941, Sec. 22, eff. Sept. 1, 2001.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The operator or owner of a vehicle is not required to unload any part of the vehicle’s load under Section 621.403 (Unloading Vehicle if Gross Weight Exceeded) or 621.404 (Unloading Vehicle if Axle Weight Exceeded) if the vehicle is:
a motor vehicle loaded with timber, pulp wood, or agricultural products in their natural state being transported from the place of production to the place of marketing or first processing; or
a vehicle crossing a highway as provided by Subchapter C (Contract Allowing Oversize or Overweight Vehicle to Cross Road; Surety Bond) , Chapter 623 (Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles) .

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The operator of a motor vehicle may proceed to the vehicle’s destination without unloading the vehicle as required by Section 621.403 (Unloading Vehicle if Gross Weight Exceeded) or 621.404 (Unloading Vehicle if Axle Weight Exceeded) if the vehicle is loaded with livestock.
